Descriptions: The RetroDiscoTable consists of a surface which holds 48 tiles and a bottom part where a speaker is built in.
In every tile, there are 3 RGB-LEDs which can be individually controlled by a remote app.
The app uses Bluetooth to communicate with the Arduino board which is built into the bottom part of the table.
You can play songs and tunes which are stored on a sd card or you can start a program which automatically lights up the tiles in specific patterns.
If you want to go for a more interactive way you can play a game (e.g. snake) or you can draw a picture by selecting color and tile.

I developed two versions of the app. One that has the logic code on the Arduino board (instructions get executed faster but is harder to debug, app sends start/stop commands) and one that has the logic code in the app (slower instructions execution but easier to debug, app sends tile on/off commands).
The Motuino is a small vehicle which consists of two controllable wheels at the back and one support wheel at the front.
You can control it with any Bluetooth control app from the app store.
The Arduino board rests on top of the vehicle and accepts string commands and translate them to mechanical movement.
The Motuino automatically avoids obstacles with its built in distance sensor. It receives its energy through four AAA batteries which are located at the back of the vehicle.
